perturbation.original_text: Signal NDCA 20160308 1 Non Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement This Confidentiality Agreement (“Agreement”), entered into as of ________ _____ (“Effective Date”), is by and between Signal Energy, LLC, (including its affiliates , EMJ Corporation, Signal Wind Energy , LLC and Signal Solar , LLC ), having its principal offices at 2034 Hamilton Place Blvd., Suite 400, Chattanooga, TN 37421 (“Company”), and ____________________ ___________________________ _ and its affiliates, (“Counterparty”).
perturbation.changed_text: Signal NDCA 20160308 1 Non Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement This Confidentiality Agreement (“Agreement”), entered into as of ________ _____ (“Effective Date”), is by and between Signal Energy, LLC, (including its affiliates , EMJ Corporation, Signal Wind Energy , LLC and Signal Solar , LLC ), having its principal offices at 2034 Hamilton Place Blvd., Suite 400, Chattanooga, TN 37421 (“Company”), and ____________________ ___________________________ _ and its affiliates, (“Service Provider”).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Counterparty' to 'Service Provider' creates ambiguity. While context implies they are the same, the agreement does not explicitly state it. This might lead to confusion regarding obligations specifically assigned to the 'Counterparty' later in the agreement, potentially creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, Company and Counterparty shall each be referred to as a “Party” and collectively as the “Parties”; WHEREAS the Parties desire to hold discussions and exchange information regarding Counterparty’s equipment and/or services to Company related to wind energy facilities, solar en ergy facilities, energy facilities, business evaluation, funding, purchase agreement or other business transaction (collectively the “Transaction s”); WHEREAS, in the course of evaluating the Transactions it will be necessary for Company (“Disclosing Party”) t o release certain Confidential Information (as defined below) to the Counterparty (“Receiving Party”);
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the second party as 'Counterparty'. The changed text replaces 'Counterparty' with 'Service Provider'. The 'Contradicted Text' defines the roles of 'Company' and 'Counterparty' regarding the exchange of confidential information. By changing 'Counterparty' to 'Service Provider', it creates an ambiguity and potential contradiction because the agreement now has two different terms ('Counterparty' and 'Service Provider') referring to the same entity, without explicitly stating they are interchangeable. This ambiguity makes it unclear whether the obligations and rights assigned to 'Counterparty' in the 'Contradicted Text' also apply to the 'Service Provider', leading to an in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, in the course of evaluating the Transactions it will be necessary for Company (“Disclosing Party”) t o release certain Confidential Information (as defined below) to the Counterparty (“Receiving Party”);
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, in the course of evaluating the Transactions it will be necessary for Company (“Disclosing Party”) t o release certain Confidential Information (as defined below) to the Service Provider (“Beneficiary”);
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Beneficiary' after previously changing 'Counterparty' to 'Service Provider' introduces further contradiction. The roles are no longer aligned because, throughout the agreement, Receiving Party is used for the role that Counterparty is playing, but these have been changed, leading to an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Nondisclosure and Use of Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall not be used for any purpose other than to analyze, implement or complet e the Transactions . Confidential Information shall be held in strict confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be disclosed without prior written consent of Disclosing Party, except to those advisors, affiliates, agents, assigns, attorneys, employees, d irectors, officers and/or members (“Agents”) with a need -to-know the Confidential Information for the purposes of analyzing, implementing or completing the Transactions . Receiving Party shall require all recipients of the Confidential Information to be bo und by the terms of this Agreement. Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Agents. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as the Receiving Party employs to protect its own information of like importance, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care based on industry standard.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Receiving Party' as the Counterparty that receives confidential information. The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Beneficiary' while the 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'Receiving Party' having specific obligations regarding confidential information. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now uses 'Receiving Party' in a section expecting the counterparty to fulfill duties, but the original definition of 'Receiving Party' as Counterparty has been replaced, leading to confusion about who is responsible for those duties. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition because it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them consistently, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall be held in strict confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be disclosed without prior written consent of Disclosing Party, except to those advisors, affiliates, agents, assigns, attorneys, employees, d irectors, officers and/or members (“Agents”) with a need -to-know the Confidential Information for the purposes of analyzing, implementing or completing the Transactions . Receiving Party shall require all recipients of the Confidential Information to be bo und by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall be held in strict confidence by the Beneficiary and shall not be disclosed without prior written consent of Disclosing Party, except to those advisors, affiliates, agents, assigns, attorneys, employees, d irectors, officers and/or members (“Agents”) with a need -to-know the Confidential Information for the purposes of analyzing, implementing or completing the Transactions . Service Provider shall require all recipients of the Confidential Information to be bo und by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By having 'Beneficiary' and 'Service Provider' used in place of 'Receiving Party' in the same section but for different clauses it creates an in-text contradiction by making it unclear if they have the same obligations. Service Provider is also not defined as the Beneficiary agent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Nondisclosure and Use of Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall not be used for any purpose other than to analyze, implement or complet e the Transactions . Confidential Information shall be held in strict confidence by the Beneficiary and shall not be disclosed without prior written consent of Disclosing Party, except to those advisors, affiliates, agents, assigns, attorneys, employees, d irectors, officers and/or members (“Agents”) with a need -to-know the Confidential Information for the purposes of analyzing, implementing or completing the Transactions . Service Provider shall require all recipients of the Confidential Information to be bo und by the terms of this Agreement. Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Agents. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as the Receiving Party employs to protect its own information of like importance, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care based on industry standard.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Beneficiary' in one clause and 'Service Provider' in another within the same section, while the contradicted text uses all three terms ('Beneficiary', 'Service Provider', and 'Receiving Party') but only defines the Receiving Party's responsibilities ('Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach'). This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether 'Beneficiary' and 'Service Provider' have the same obligations as 'Receiving Party', leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability, and fitting the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as the Receiving Party employs to protect its own information of like importance, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care based on industry standard.
perturbation.changed_text: The Service Provider shall use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as the Beneficiary employs to protect its own information of like importance, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care based on industry standard.
perturbation.explanation: Using 'Service Provider' and 'Beneficiary' here, while 'Receiving Party' is still used previously in the same section, creates an in-text contradiction because the 'Service Provider' isn't identified as the party receiving the information, so it isn't clear which entity has the responsibility to protect the information. The original text holds Receiving Party accountable but this is now replaced. The text is now contradictive because there are three different parties that have different meanings but are assigned the same responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Nondisclosure and Use of Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall not be used for any purpose other than to analyze, implement or complet e the Transactions . Confidential Information shall be held in strict confidence by the Beneficiary and shall not be disclosed without prior written consent of Disclosing Party, except to those advisors, affiliates, agents, assigns, attorneys, employees, d irectors, officers and/or members (“Agents”) with a need -to-know the Confidential Information for the purposes of analyzing, implementing or completing the Transactions . Service Provider shall require all recipients of the Confidential Information to be bo und by the terms of this Agreement. Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Agents. The Service Provider shall use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as the Beneficiary employs to protect its own information of like importance, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care based on industry standard.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces 'Service Provider' and 'Beneficiary' while the contradicted text refers to 'Receiving Party'. The explanation states this creates a contradiction because it's unclear which entity is responsible for protecting the information, especially since 'Receiving Party' is still used elsewhere. This misalignment of terminology directly conflicts with the contradicted text, where Receiving Party's responsibilities are explicitly stated. The change makes enforcement unclear and creates a logical inconsistency about who is responsible for the breach, hence 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Beneficiary agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Substituting 'Receiving Party' for 'Beneficiary' in this remedies section creates in-text contradiction because it leaves the obligations from Section 2 to fall under 'Receiving Party' but the liabilities under 'Beneficiary,' potentially limiting the Disclosing Party's remedies. The 'Receiving Party' and 'Beneficiary' were originally defined as the same party but now they are not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Remedies. The Beneficiary agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall not be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, the Receiving Party’s liability to the Disclosing Party in connection with this Agreement and any activities undertaken in connection with the evaluation of the Transactions shall be limite d to direct damages and shall exclude any other liability, including without limitation liability for special, indirect, punitive or consequential damages in contract, tort, warranty, strict liability or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Beneficiary'. The contradicted text explicitly refers to both 'Beneficiary' and 'Receiving Party', assigning different remedies/liabilities to each. By changing one instance of 'Receiving Party' to 'Beneficiary', the changed text creates a contradiction because the liabilities originally assigned to the Receiving Party in the contradicted text now become ambiguous, as the obligations from Section 2 fall under Receiving Party but the liabilities under Beneficiary, potentially limiting the Disclosing Party's remedies. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, the Receiving Party’s liability to the Disclosing Party in connection with this Agreement and any activities undertaken in connection with the evaluation of the Transactions shall be limite d to direct damages and shall exclude any other liability, including without limitation liability for special, indirect, punitive or consequential damages in contract, tort, warranty, strict liability or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, the Service Provider’s liability to the Disclosing Party in connection with this Agreement and any activities undertaken in connection with the evaluation of the Transactions shall be limite d to direct damages and shall exclude any other liability, including without limitation liability for special, indirect, punitive or consequential damages in contract, tort, warranty, strict liability or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Service Provider' limits their liability but does not address or change who is receiving the information (Beneficiary). The agreement is now contradictive about which party is liable for a breach in relation to what they receive/disclose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return or Destruction. Promptly fol lowing any decision by the Receiving Party not to continue discussions with respect to the Transactions , and at any other time upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party shall return or destroy, at the Receiving Party’s option, all wr itten Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, including that portion of such Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by, or for, the Receiving Party, and the Receiving Party and it s Agents shall not retain any copies of such written Confidential Information. At any time after which the Receiving Party has been required to return or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession in accordance with the preceding sentence, the Receiving Party shall, upon written request of the Disclosing Party, cause one of its duly authorized officers to certify in writing to the Disclosing Party that the requirements of the preceding sentence have Signal NDCA 20160308 3 been satisfied in full. The Receiving Party shall not be deemed to have retained or failed to destroy any Confidential Information which is an Imaged Document if such Confidential Information is deleted from local hard drives so long as no attempt is made to recover such Confidential Information fro m servers or back -up sources, provided that any such retained Confidential Information in an Imaged Document form shall remain subject to the disclosure and use restrictions set forth herein, notwithstanding any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Receiving Party' and limits their liability. The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Service Provider' creating a contradiction because the agreement is no longer clear about who is liable for a breach related to the received or disclosed information. The 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'Receiving Party' having certain obligations, and by changing the liable party, a contradiction arises about which party must fulfill these obligations.
